title: Reading Literary Texts
teksDescription: Develop and sustain an appropriate response to literary and informational texts through a variety of modes, including written, oral, and visual.

title: Reading Informational Texts
teksDescription: analyze how the organizational structure of an expository text contributes to the author's purpose and message

title: Reading Persuasive Texts
teksDescription: analyze rhetorical devices and logical fallacies used in arguments

title: Author's Craft and Style
teksDescription: analyze how the author's use of diction, syntax, imagery, figurative language, and tone creates a desired effect

title: Vocabulary and Academic Language
teksDescription: Develop vocabulary by using context clues, knowledge of word origins, and other resources.

title: Writing Process and Composition
teksDescription: Plan a first draft by selecting a genre appropriate for a particular purpose and audience and then generating a plan that sets out ideas, logic, and a sequence of information.

title: Research and Synthesis
teksDescription: Develop and demonstrate an understanding of the research process, including how to select and narrow a topic, formulate a research question, gather relevant information from a variety of sources, evaluate the credibility and reliability of sources, synthesize information from multiple sources, and cite sources appropriately.

title: Oral and Written Conventions
teksDescription: Students are expected to use and understand the conventions of academic writing in English, including grammar, punctuation, and spelling.

title: Poetry and Drama
teksDescription: analyze the aesthetic effects of literary devices and elements, including figurative language, imagery, symbolism, sound devices, and allusions, in poetry, drama, and prose; compare and contrast the use of literary devices and elements in various genres

title: Cross-Genre Comparison
teksDescription: Students analyze how the author's purpose and the use of literary devices and rhetorical appeals influence the reader's interpretation of texts in various genres.

title: Media and Visual Literacy
teksDescription: evaluate the uses and effects of rhetorical devices, literary elements, and literary techniques in various forms of media, including informational texts, speeches, and literary texts

title: STAAR ENG2 Exam Review
teksDescription: Students are expected to synthesize information from a variety of sources to create a coherent understanding of a topic.

title: Reconstruction Era
teksDescription: evaluate the impact of the election of Andrew Johnson, congressional Reconstruction, and the impeachment of Johnson on the Reconstruction Era

title: Industrialisation and the Gilded Age
teksDescription: Analyze the impact of significant technological innovations and the rise of big business, the growth of industry, and the development of the free enterprise system during the Gilded Age.

title: Progressive Era
teksDescription: Identify the causes and effects of the Progressive Movement.

title: World War I and the 1920s
teksDescription: Analyze the causes and effects of World War I on the United States, including the reasons for U.S. entry into the war, the impact of significant individuals, and the Treaty of Versailles.

title: Great Depression and New Deal
teksDescription: Analyze the causes of the Great Depression, including the Dust Bowl.

title: World War II
teksDescription: Identify reasons for U.S. involvement in World War II, including the attack on Pearl Harbor.

title: Cold War Era
teksDescription: Identify the causes and effects of the Cold War on American society, including the arms race, McCarthyism, and the Space Race.

title: Civil Rights Movement
teksDescription: Explain the significance of the following events, policies, and individuals on the Civil Rights Movement: Brown v. Board of Education, the Civil Rights Act of 1964, the Voting Rights Act of 1965, and Martin Luther King, Jr.

title: Vietnam War and Social Change
teksDescription: Identify the causes and effects of the Vietnam War, including the domino theory, the Gulf of Tonkin Resolution, the Tet Offensive, and the role of the media.

title: Contemporary America 1980s–2001
teksDescription: Evaluate the impact of U.S. foreign policy on other nations and on the global economy during the late 20th and early 21st centuries, including the end of the Cold War, the Persian Gulf War, and the War on Terror.

title: 21st-Century America
teksDescription: Identify and explain the causes and effects of the September 11, 2001 terrorist attacks on the United States and the subsequent War on Terror.

title: STAAR USH Exam Review
teksDescription: Recall, analyze, and apply all United States history since 1877 TEKS.
